    <script>
      // Resolve theme from (1) signed-in user's saved preference, (2) falling
      // back to the OS color-scheme. Applied before first paint to avoid FOUC.
      // When preference === 'system', live-updates as the OS preference changes.
      (function () {
        var root = document.documentElement;
        var meta = document.querySelector('meta[name="theme-preference"]');
        var pref = (meta && meta.getAttribute('content')) || 'system';
        var mq = window.matchMedia('(prefers-color-scheme: dark)');
        function apply() {
          var current = (document.querySelector('meta[name="theme-preference"]')?.getAttribute('content')) || 'system';
          var dark = current === 'dark' || (current === 'system' && mq.matches);
          root.classList.toggle('dark', dark);
        }
        apply();
        mq.addEventListener('change', apply);
        // Expose so the React shell can re-apply after saving a new preference.
        window.__applyThemePreference = apply;
      })();
    </script>

    #nprogress {
      pointer-events: none;
    }

    #nprogress .bar {
      background: #29d;

      position: fixed;
      z-index: 1031;
      top: 0;
      left: 0;

      width: 100%;
      height: 2px;
    }

    #nprogress .peg {
      display: block;
      position: absolute;
      right: 0px;
      width: 100px;
      height: 100%;
      box-shadow: 0 0 10px #29d, 0 0 5px #29d;
      opacity: 1.0;

      transform: rotate(3deg) translate(0px, -4px);
    }

    #nprogress .spinner {
      display: block;
      position: fixed;
      z-index: 1031;
      top: 15px;
      right: 15px;
    }

    #nprogress .spinner-icon {
      width: 18px;
      height: 18px;
      box-sizing: border-box;

      border: solid 2px transparent;
      border-top-color: #29d;
      border-left-color: #29d;
      border-radius: 50%;

      animation: nprogress-spinner 400ms linear infinite;
    }

    .nprogress-custom-parent {
      overflow: hidden;
      position: relative;
    }

    .nprogress-custom-parent #nprogress .spinner,
    .nprogress-custom-parent #nprogress .bar {
      position: absolute;
    }

    @keyframes nprogress-spinner {
      0%   { transform: rotate(0deg); }
      100% { transform: rotate(360deg); }
    }

is Agent OS?</h2><p>Every time you prompt an AI coding agent, youÔÇÖre re-teaching it context that should already be known. The reasoning behind your conventions, the patterns your team refined over time, the architectural decisions that shape everything ÔÇö none of that comes through when an agent scans your code.</p><p>Agent OS fills that gap. ItÔÇÖs a lightweight system for defining and managing your coding standards in AI-powered development ÔÇö and in v3, it discovers and documents those standards for you.</p><p>ItÔÇÖs designed to complement tools like Claude Code, Cursor, and other AI coding assistants, not replace them.</p><h3>Key benefits</h3><ul><li><strong>Standards management</strong> ÔÇö Maintain coding standards across all agentic work. Whether youÔÇÖre writing specs, prompting agents, or creating custom Skills, inject your standards to keep work aligned.</li><li><strong>Legacy codebase support</strong> ÔÇö Bring pre-AI codebases into the modern era. Discover and document tribal knowledge that exists only in your code, making it easier to use AI agents going forward.</li><li><strong>Better spec shaping</strong> ÔÇö Enhanced shaping questions (run in plan mode) help you create stronger, more aligned specs that account for your productÔÇÖs mission and all your standards.</li><li><strong>Team alignment</strong> ÔÇö Keep your entire organization aligned on a spec-driven approach with Claude Code or any AI coding tool.</li></ul><h3>How it works</h3><ol><li><a href="/agent-os/installation">Install</a> ÔÇö Set up Agent OS in your project with a simple installation script</li><li><a href="/agent-os/discover-standards">Discover</a> ÔÇö Extract existing patterns from your codebase into documented standards</li><li><a href="/agent-os/inject-standards">Inject</a> ÔÇö Deploy relevant standards into your context when you need them</li><li><a href="/agent-os/shape-spec">Shape</a> ÔÇö Use enhanced shaping in plan mode to create specs aligned with your standards</li></ol><h3>Works with any tool</h3><p>Agent OS is designed primarily for Claude Code, with slash commands that integrate directly into your workflow. However, since all outputs are markdown files, Agent OS works just as well with any AI coding tool:</p><ul><li><strong>Claude Code</strong> (recommended) ÔÇö Full integration with slash commands</li><li>Cursor</li><li>Windsurf</li><li>Codex</li><li>Any AI coding assistant that can read files</li></ul><p>For tools other than Claude Code, simply reference the underlying files in <code>agent-os/</code> directly.</p><h3>Next steps</h3><ul><li><a class="arrow-link" href="/agent-os/installation"><span>Installation guide</span><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="1em"
